plainEnglish: When an element receives keyboard focus, it must not be entirely hidden behind other content you've added—like a sticky header, footer, or cookie banner. At minimum the focused element has to be at least partially visible so keyboard users can see where they are.
whyItMatters: Keyboard users lose track of focus when the focused element scrolls completely behind a sticky header, footer, or floating banner.
quickCheck: Tab through the page with sticky headers and footers present and confirm the focused element is never completely hidden behind them.
commonMistakes: Sticky headers or footers that cover the focused element as you tab down the page.
Cookie banners or chat widgets that overlap focused controls.
Not adding scroll-margin so anchored or focused elements clear fixed overlays.

codeExamples.label: Scroll-margin keeps focus clear of the sticky header
Sticky header hides elements as they get focus
codeExamples.code:
/* No scroll-margin: focused elements scroll under the header */
.site-header { position: sticky; top: 0; height: 6rem; }
